name: Bhagoria Festival
description: Bhil tribal festival where young men and women choose their life partners through song and dance before Holi.

name: Madai Festival
description: Ancient Gondi tribal festival with deity processions, traditional dances and indigenous crafts in dense forests.

name: Bastar Dussehra
description: The world's longest festival lasting 75 days — unique Gondi tribal version of Dussehra unlike any other in India.

name: Rajim Kumbh Mela
description: Chhattisgarh's answer to Kumbh Mela at the sacred confluence of three rivers with sadhus and tribal rituals.

name: Hareli Festival
description: Chhattisgarh's first festival of the year worshipping agricultural tools before sowing season begins.

name: Medaram Jatara
description: World's largest tribal festival with 8 crore pilgrims visiting the forest shrine of Sammakka and Sarakka.

name: Bonalu
description: Telangana's vibrant goddess festival with women carrying food offerings on their heads to Mahakali temples.

name: Bathukamma
description: Telangana women stack seasonal flowers into cone-shaped arrangements and immerse them in water in this unique festival.

name: Karam Festival
description: Jharkhand tribal festival worshipping the Karam tree with songs and dances praying for prosperity and good harvest.

name: Sarhul
description: Jharkhand's spring festival where Munda and Oraon tribes worship the Sal tree with dancing and brewing rice beer.

name: Karma Festival
description: Adivasi festival of nature worship where women fast and men bring Karma tree branches for elaborate rituals.

name: Moatsu Festival
description: Ao Naga tribe's post-sowing festival with feasting, singing, folk dances and traditional warrior performances.

name: Sekrenyi Festival
description: Angami Naga purification festival with elaborate rituals, feasting and traditional warrior dances.

name: Ngada Festival
description: Garo tribe's winter festival celebrating the harvest with traditional songs, dances and community feasting.

name: Behdienkhlam
description: Jaintia tribe's most important festival with decorated bamboo towers carried through streets to drive away plague.

name: Nongkrem Dance Festival
description: Khasi tribe's sacred festival with men dancing in war attire and women in silver and silk performing ancient dances.

name: Porag Festival
description: Bodo tribe's spring festival celebrating love and new beginnings with traditional music and courtship rituals.

name: Ali Ai Ligang
description: Mising tribe's seed sowing festival on the world's largest river island with traditional rice beer and dances.

name: Dehing Patkai Festival
description: Multi-ethnic festival in Assam's tea country celebrating the diverse cultures of Upper Assam tribes.

name: Garo Wangala
description: 100-drum festival of the Garo tribe thanking the Sun God for a good harvest with days of music and dance.

name: Pung Cholom
description: Manipuri drum dance festival where performers play the Pung drum while executing acrobatic dance movements.

name: Yaoshang
description: Manipur's Holi equivalent lasting five days with Thabal Chongba moonlit dance and traditional sports.

name: Lui-Ngai-Ni
description: Naga seed sowing festival in Manipur with all tribes united in celebration before the agricultural season.

name: Chapchar Kut
description: Mizoram's biggest festival with bamboo dances and traditional Mizo songs celebrating the clearing of jungle for cultivation.

name: Mim Kut
description: Mizo maize harvest festival dedicated to the spirits of the dead with feasting and traditional songs.

name: Pavam Mahotsav
description: Kerala tribal festival bringing together indigenous communities from the Nilgiris for traditional arts and healing rituals.

name: Attuvela Mahotsavam
description: Unique Kerala festival where giant floats carrying hundreds of lamps are paraded around a temple all night.

name: Kottiyoor Vysakha Mahotsavam
description: Remote Kerala temple festival accessible only by crossing a river on foot — no vehicles allowed — for 28 days.

name: Theyyam
description: Ancient ritualistic art form where performers become living gods — one of India's most extraordinary spiritual experiences.

name: Kodungallur Bharani
description: Kerala's most intense and controversial temple festival with devotees singing explicit folk songs to the goddess.

name: Kila Raipur Sports Festival
description: India's Rural Olympics with bullock cart races, tug of war and acrobatics by trained horses and bulls.

name: Baisakhi Mela Anandpur Sahib
description: Grand Sikh festival at the birthplace of the Khalsa with massive gatherings and Hola Mohalla martial displays.

name: Hola Mohalla
description: Sikh festival of mock battles, martial arts demonstrations and Nihang warriors displaying their skills on horseback.

name: Kullu Dussehra
description: International festival where 200 local deities descend from Himalayan villages to meet the god Raghunath in procession.

name: Minjar Fair
description: Ancient Himachal fair marking the start of the maize harvest with a procession throwing maize tassels into the Ravi River.

name: Shivratri Fair Mandi
description: Himachal's biggest fair with 200 local deities arriving on palanquins for a week-long celebration in the Beas valley.

name: Ladarcha Fair
description: Ancient trade fair in remote Spiti where nomads from Ladakh, Kinnaur and Tibet gathered to exchange goods.

name: Surajkund Crafts Mela
description: Asia's largest crafts fair bringing master artisans from all states to showcase India's incredible handicraft traditions.

name: Jhiri Mela
description: Jammu's ancient fair at the village where a farmer was martyred for refusing to convert religion centuries ago.

name: Bundi Utsav
description: Colorful festival in India's most authentic medieval Rajput town with camel polo and traditional performing arts.

name: Marwar Festival
description: Rajasthani folk music and dance festival honoring Marwar's historic heroes under the Blue City's moonlight.

name: Brij Mahotsav
description: Classical music and dance festival in the land of Radha-Krishna near Vrindavan celebrating Braj's folk traditions.

name: Nagaur Fair
description: Second largest cattle fair in India with bullock fights, camel decoration contests and Rajasthani folk performers.

name: Teej in Jaipur
description: Monsoon festival of Rajasthani women with decorated goddess idol procession, swing rides and green attire.

name: Makar Mela Sonepur
description: Asia's largest livestock fair with elephants, horses, birds and cattle traded at the Ganges-Gandak confluence.

name: Chhath Puja
description: Bihar's most important festival where devotees stand in rivers at sunrise and sunset offering prayers to the Sun God.

name: Sonepur Mela
description: Month-long fair at the holy Harigadhi ghat with traditional wrestlers, snake charmers and elephant bazaar.

name: Puri Beach Festival
description: Sand art festival on Puri's golden beach with international sand sculptors creating massive beach artworks.

name: Konark Dance Festival
description: Classical dance festival against the backdrop of the 13th century Sun Temple — one of India's most magical settings.

name: Rath Yatra
description: Lord Jagannath's annual chariot procession where millions pull massive wooden chariots through Puri's streets.

name: Khajuraho Dance Festival
description: Classical dance performances against the UNESCO World Heritage erotic temples — a uniquely spectacular setting.

name: Lokrang Festival
description: National tribal and folk arts festival showcasing the incredible diversity of India's indigenous performing traditions.

name: Tansen Music Festival
description: Classical music festival at the tomb of Tansen — the greatest musician of Akbar's court — in the fort city.

name: Mamallapuram Dance Festival
description: Month-long classical dance festival with Bharatanatyam and other forms performed against ancient Pallava rock carvings.

name: Thyagaraja Aradhana
description: Annual carnatic music festival where thousands of musicians gather to sing in unison at the saint-composer's samadhi.

name: Natyanjali Dance Festival
description: Dance offering to Lord Nataraja at Chidambaram's ancient Shiva temple — the cosmic dancer's own abode.

name: Pongal in Thanjavur
description: Tamil harvest festival with elaborate kolam floor drawings and the famous Jallikattu bull taming in Thanjavur's villages.

name: Maa Danteshwari Fair
description: Gondi tribal pilgrimage to the fierce goddess Danteshwari with ancient buffalo sacrifice rituals in deep Bastar.

name: Gangasagar Mela
description: Makar Sankranti pilgrimage where a million Hindus bathe at the Ganges-Ocean confluence at the world's end.

name: Poush Mela
description: Four-day fair at Rabindranath Tagore's ashram with Baul mystic music, Santali tribal dance and village handicrafts.

name: Kenduli Mela
description: Annual gathering of Baul mystic singers at the birthplace of Jayadeva on Makar Sankranti — a musical pilgrimage.

name: Doljatra
description: Bengali Holi celebrated uniquely with Gaudiya Vaishnav traditions in the birthplace of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu.

name: Tusu Festival
description: Kurmi tribal girls' festival where beautifully decorated Tusu idols are immersed in rivers amid singing and dancing.

name: Ambubachi Mela
description: Tantric festival celebrating the goddess's menstruation at Kamakhya temple with sadhus from across India.

name: Bohag Bihu
description: Assam's most joyous festival welcoming the Assamese New Year with Bihu dances, buffalo fights and traditional songs.

name: Majuli Island Festival
description: Cultural festival on the world's largest river island celebrating Vaishnavite Sattra traditions with mask dances.

name: Yellamma Jatre
description: Karnataka's massive pilgrimage to the mother goddess Yellamma with over a million devotees attending across three days.

name: Mysuru Dasara Torchlight Parade
description: The grand finale of Mysuru Dasara with 50,000 armed forces personnel marching past the illuminated palace.

name: Ugadi
description: Telugu and Kannada New Year celebrated with the symbolic pachadi dish of six tastes representing life's flavors.

name: Karaga Festival
description: Ancient Thigala community festival where the priest carries a sacred pot on his head walking 30km through the city.

name: Goa Shigmo
description: Goa's spring festival with colourful floats depicting Hindu mythology parading through towns with folk dancers.

name: Chavath Festival
description: Goa's unique Ganesh Chaturthi with 5-day celebrations in Hindu temples contrasting with the Portuguese-heritage villages.

name: Nagpanchami
description: Only place in India where live cobras are worshipped by hand during this snake festival in a small Maharashtra village.

name: Ellora Festival
description: Classical music and dance performances held within the UNESCO-listed Ellora Caves complex — breathtakingly atmospheric.

name: Ganesh Chaturthi Mumbai
description: Ten-day spectacular where 150,000 Ganesh idols are installed across Mumbai before massive immersion processions.

name: Narali Purnima
description: Koli fishing community offers coconuts to the sea goddess Kadalamma marking the end of the monsoon fishing ban.

name: Pune Festival
description: Ten-day cultural festival across Pune with classical music, theatre, and folk arts celebrating Maharashtra's heritage.

name: Dahi Handi
description: Human pyramid competition where Govinda groups form towers 6-9 storeys high to break a butter pot.

name: Kalsi Mela
description: Ancient fair at the site of Emperor Ashoka's rock edicts on the Yamuna river banks — history meets festivity.

name: Nanda Devi Raj Jat
description: Once-in-12-years pilgrimage through the Himalayas with a four-horned ram leading devotees to Roopkund Lake.

name: Ganga Dussehra
description: Celebration of the Ganges descending to earth with aarti at Har Ki Pauri ghat lighting up in thousands of lamps.

name: International Yoga Festival
description: Weeklong yoga festival with world masters teaching on the banks of the Ganges in the yoga capital of the world.
